<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- Align shared config defaults in `config.js` with every entrypoint to prevent drift across CLI/CI surfaces.
- Keep refresh-plan/artifacts services decoupled by continuing to validate behavior through their dedicated unit tests.
- Three entrypoints (`cli.js`, `web.js`, `ci.js`) funnel through a common config and shared refresh services, so enforce the same option parsing contract in `config.js` before adding new flags.
- Refresh flows already have targeted unit tests, so expand them when adding new artifact types rather than creating ad-hoc scripts.
